entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: The crop being screened for ergot resistance

entities.name: ERGOT RESISTANCE
entities.description: The ability of sorghum to resist infection by ergot

entities.name: ARSI NEGELE
entities.description: The specific site in Ethiopia where experiments were conducted

entities.name: ETHIOPIA
entities.description: The country where the research was conducted

entities.name: PANICLE TRIMMING
entities.description: A method used to remove pollinated spikelets from sorghum panicles

entities.name: INOCULATION
entities.description: The process of introducing the ergot pathogen into the plant

entities.name: BAGGING
entities.description: The process of covering panicles after inoculation

entities.name: ERGOT RESISTANCE SCREENING TECHNIQUE
entities.description: The overall developed procedure to identify ergot-resistant sorghum lines

entities.name: VISUAL ERGOT RATING
entities.description: A rapid evaluation method using a 1-5 scale to identify susceptible genotypes

entities.name: COUNTING INFECTED AND HEALTHY SPIKELETS
entities.description: A confirmation method for resistance by analyzing primary branches of panicles

entities.name: ERGOT
entities.description: The fungal pathogen that infects sorghum spikelets

entities.name: ETHIOPIAN SORGHUM ACCESSIONS
entities.description: A group of 213 sorghum accessions from Ethiopia used for screening

entities.name: ERGOT-RESISTANT LINES
entities.description: Six specific sorghum lines (ETS 1446, 2448, 2465, 3135, 4457, and 4927) identified as resistant

entities.name: HIGHLANDS OF ETHIOPIA
entities.description: The geographic region to which the identified resistant lines are well adapted
